I need to design a main driver to hidden all details for the end user. Current document and script and command instructions are designed for developers not the end user. Make a plan to design this

attachment.content:
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Design main driver for end-user simplicity

content.text: I'll plan a unified end-user driver that hides developer-facing details. Let me first explore the codebase to understand the current state.

input.description: Explore current entry points
input.prompt:
Explore the hybrid_sim repository at /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim to understand all current user-facing entry points. I'm planning a unified "main driver" that hides developer details from end users, so I need a complete map of what exists today.

Specifically map out:

1. **All shell scripts** under `scripts/` - what each one does, its arguments, env vars, and what it produces. Especially:
   - `setup.sh`
   - `build_qemu.sh`, `build_vsim.sh`, `build_simpoint.sh`
   - `run_e2e.sh`
   - Any other top-level scripts

2. **Test harnesses** under `tests/hybrid/` and `verilator/tests/hybrid/`:
   - `e2e.sh` - what env vars / arguments it takes, what it does step-by-step (high level)
   - How fixtures are launched (QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 phases)
   - How `TRIGGER_MODE`, `MODE` (csr/pc), `JOBS`, `FILTER`, `LOG_DIR` are wired

3. **Make targets** in `tests/fixtures/Makefile` and any top-level Makefiles - what `.elf`s are built and what flags.

4. **CMake entry points** in `verilator/CMakeLists.txt` and `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - what options the user needs to set, what binaries are produced (e.g. `sim_ax45mpv_premium`).

5. **Documentation files**: list everything under `docs/` and a one-line summary of each. Highlight which docs are developer-focused vs end-user focused.

For each script/tool, report:
- Where it lives (file:line for the entrypoint)
- What inputs it needs (args, env vars, files)
- What outputs it produces
- Whether it has a `--help` or usage message
- Dependencies on other scripts/binaries

Goal: I need a punch list of every "knob" a developer currently has to know, so I can design a single command that does the right thing by default. Report under 600 words. Use bullet lists.

input.description: Explore typical end-user workflow
input.prompt:
Explore /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim to understand what a typical end-user workflow looks like for the Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator (QEMU + vsim).

I want to design a single "main driver" command that hides all the developer plumbing. To do that, I need to understand what an end user actually wants to do with this simulator. Look at:

1. **What ELF programs run on this hybrid simulator?** Survey `tests/fixtures/` and any examples. Specifically:
   - What's the difference between asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_all_gprs, rt_csr_survival, rt_shared_mem) and C-level fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_matmul, rt_c_v_regs, rt_c_v_csrs)?
   - What does a fixture need to do to be "hybrid-compatible"? (look at `tests/fixtures/runtime/` if it exists)
   - What is `enter_vsim()` / `exit_vsim()` in C, and how do asm fixtures equivalent? (search for `_hybrid_enter_pc`, `csrwi 0x7C0`)

2. **What does running one of these look like end-to-end?** Read `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` (or the verilator/tests/hybrid version) and trace what happens:
   - How many processes get spawned (QEMU1, vsim, QEMU2)?
   - What temporary files / shared mmaps get created?
   - What's the success/failure criterion?
   - What stdout/stderr is produced — is it noisy?

3. **What CPU configs exist?** Look at `verilator/config*.yaml` or similar. Are there multiple CPU variants (ax45mp, ax45mpv, premium vs non-premium)?

4. **What does the user see today if they just want to "run my ELF on the hybrid simulator"?**
   - Is there ANY one-shot command? Or do they need to know about: `setup.sh`, `make -C tests/fixtures`, `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`?
   - What's the minimum invocation today to run a single fixture? (e.g. `./tests/hybrid/e2e.sh ...`)

5. **What's in `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` and `docs/TODO.md`?** Summarize what's already documented for users and what's noted as missing.

Report under 600 words with concrete file:line citations. Be honest about what's painful for an end user today — list specific friction points (must know JOBS=8, must source setup.sh, must build fixtures separately, etc.).

input.description: Explore docs and TODO state
input.prompt:
Read these specific files in /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim verbatim and report their contents/structure:

1. `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` - full contents or, if very long, the section headings + a 2-line summary of each section.
2. `docs/TODO.md` - full contents (this is an untracked file noted in git status).
3. `docs/e2e_ROADMAP.md` if it exists - section headings + status of each feature (especially F1-F10 or however it's numbered).
4. `README.md` at repo root if it exists - full or summary.
5. List all other files under `docs/` with one-line summaries.

Also check:
6. Is there a `Makefile` at the repo root? If yes, what targets does it have?
7. Is there any existing "wrapper" command that calls into the simulator end-to-end? (Look for things like `bin/`, `cli.py`, `run.sh` at the repo root.)
8. What does `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` (or `verilator/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh`) look like at the top - what env vars does it document? What's its usage line? Read the first 80 lines and report.

I'm planning a single end-user driver, so I want to know what's already documented vs what's still TODO. Report concisely (under 500 words), with bullet lists and file:line citations.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information. Let me compile the report:

---

## Documentation and Tooling Summary

### **Documented user-facing guidance**

1. **`docs/USER_GUIDE.md`** (fully comprehensive, 693 lines)
   - Sections 0–7: one-paragraph model, build/env setup, configuration, switching modes (M1–M8), walk-through exercises (M1–M7 + T7 demo chain), full test suite, common failures
   - **Audience:** new engineer who wants to RUN, not study internals
   - **Status:** Complete; covers all trigger modes and concrete worked examples

2. **`docs/TODO.md`** (untracked, comprehensive failure-modes audit; 317 lines)
   - **Format:** 16 tracked failure modes (F-T1.1–F-T3.8), split Tier 1 (will fail on next workload) / Tier 2 (scaling) / Tier 3 (invisible bugs)
   - **Examples:** semihosting inside ROI (F-T1.1), FP/PMP state not restored (F-T1.2–F-T1.3, marked DONE in F-T1.2), exception state lost (F-T1.4), LR/SC reservation (F-T1.5)
   - **Status:** F-T1.2 (FP) & F-T2.4 (mcycle monotonicity) marked DONE; others not-started
   - **Highest-leverage next:** Add register-readback assertions to T3–T7 demos; promote Phase 5 hardening to tracked ROADMAP

3. **`docs/e2e_ROADMAP.md`** — **DOES NOT EXIST**

4. **`docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`** — Architecture deep-dive (mentioned in USER_GUIDE §7)

5. **`docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`** — Per-mode drill-down exercises

6. **`docs/v_csr_plan.md`** — Full V-CSR support (in-flight TDD sequence)

7. **`docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md`** — Semihosting gap analysis (unsolved)

8. **`docs/simpoint_transport_comparison.md`** — mmap vs dump/reload trade-offs

### **Build/test entry points (no single CLI driver yet)**

- **`setup.sh`** — Sources config, builds all 4 artifacts (QEMU, vsim, plugin, SimPoint), exports env vars; called before everything
- **`tests/e2e.sh`** — Generic harness for QEMU1 → vsim → QEMU2 round-trip; requires `FIXTURE` env var; respects `TRIGGER_MODE` (csr|pc), `TIMEOUT`, `EXPECT_RC`; documented in lines 1–18
- **`scripts/run_e2e.sh`** — Parallel test runner; respects `JOBS` (cap at 8 on this 128-core host), `MODE` (csr|pc), `FILTER` (regex), `LOG_DIR`
- **`tests/demos/demo_*.sh`** — M1–T7 demos (demo_csr_oneshot, demo_pc_oneshot, demo_icount_roundtrip, demo_bbv_profile, demo_slice_consume_roundtrip, demo_archive_replay, demo_simpoint_loop)
- **No `Makefile` at root** — `tests/fixtures/Makefile` builds ELF fixtures
- **No `bin/`, `cli.py`, or unified wrapper** at repo root

### **Configuration surfaces documented**

| Layer | File | Scope |
|-------|------|-------|
| Per-host paths | `config.env.example` → `config.env` (gitignored) | Toolchain root; auto-copied on first `source ./setup.sh` |
| Artifacts | `setup.sh` exports | `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`, `VSIM_ELF`, `SIMPOINT_BIN`, `NM`, `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` |
| CPU model | `verilator/config.yaml` | Andes core choice (default: ax45mpv_premium); select via CMake `-DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE` |
| Plugin args | QEMU `-plugin` flag | `outfile=`, `enter_pc=`, `icount=`, `bbv_out=`, `slice=`, `slice_at=` |
| vsim CLI | Direct flags | `--resume-from-qemu`, `--handoff-out`, `--shared-mem-*`, `--hybrid-exit-pc`, `--hybrid-icount`, etc. |
| Multi-step plans | YAML (`steps:` array) + orchestrator | M6/M7 checkpoint workflows (currently minimal documentation) |

### **Test suite structure**

- E2E via `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with env vars (`JOBS`, `FILTER`, `MODE`)
- CTest registered in `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (gated by `-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`)
- Demos in `tests/demos/` (7 files: M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, M6, T7)
- Fixture demos in `tests/fixtures/demo.sh` (single-test sequential runner)

### **Gap for end-user driver**

**What exists:**
- Every trigger mode is thoroughly documented with copy-pastable examples (USER_GUIDE §3–4)
- Environment variables are standardized
- Common failures are catalogued (USER_GUIDE §6)

**What's missing:**
- No single unified `run` / `sim` / `execute` command that hides orchestration complexity
- No interactive or programmatic entry point that bundles {artifact lookup, env setup, trigger selection, shared mmap creation, result reporting}
- No ROADMAP file tracking high-level completion state (F1–F10 style)
- Demos are shell scripts (not a CLI); demos don't validate register state at QEMU2 entry (TODO: F-T3.1)

---

**Recommendation:** A new end-user driver should accept {ELF path, trigger mode (M1–M8), optional config YAML} and return {exit code, state.bin path, result summary}. It can wrap `tests/e2e.sh` + orchestrator, but isolate env setup, mmap lifecycle, and error reporting from the user.

lastPrompt: I need to design a main driver to hidden all details for the end user. Current document and script and command instructions are designed for developers not the end user. Make a plan to design this

aiTitle: Design main driver for end-user simplicity

content.text:
Now I have enough information. Let me compile the comprehensive map.

## Complete Entry Points Map for hybrid_sim

### 1. Shell Scripts (`scripts/`)

**setup.sh** (root level)
- **Location:** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/setup.sh`
- **Usage:** `source ./setup.sh [clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]]`
- **Inputs:** `config.env` (auto-created from template), `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` env var
- **Outputs:** Exports `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`, `SIMPOINT_BIN`, `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN`; prepends `build/qemu` and `verilator/build` to `PATH`
- **Flow:** Steps 0-4 (config → qemu → vsim → plugin → simpoint)
- **Clean support:** Full component-wise cleanup

**build_qemu.sh**
- **Location:** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/scripts/build_qemu.sh`
- **Inputs:** QEMU submodule at `QEMU/`, default configure flags
- **Outputs:** `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- **Args:** None; uses `-j64`
- **Requirements:** `qemu/configure` (fails if submodule not initialized)

**build_vsim.sh**
- **Location:** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/scripts/build_vsim.sh`
- **Inputs:** `verilator/Dockerfile` (target: `env`), `verilator/build.sh` inside container
- **Outputs:** Container builds; final binary: `verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (CPU-dependent)
- **Mechanism:** Podman image `vsim-env:latest`; mounts `/work` and `/include:ro`
- **Dependencies:** Verilator, CMake, ninja (inside container)

**build_simpoint.sh**
- **Location:** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/scripts/build_simpoint.sh`
- **Inputs:** `tools/simpoint/Makefile`
- **Outputs:** `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
- **Build:** `make -C $SRC Simpoint` + idempotent copy to `bin/simpoint`

**run_e2e.sh**
- **Location:** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/scripts/run_e2e.sh`
- **Env Vars Accepted:**
  - `VSIM_QEMU` - path to QEMU binary (default: `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`)
  - `VSIM_PLUGIN` - path to plugin .so (default: `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`)
  - `VSIM_BIN` - path to vsim binary (default: first `sim_*` in `verilator/build/`)
  - `FILTER` - regex to subset test names
  - `MODE` - `csr|pc|both` (default: `both`) - controls trigger modes per RT_TESTS
  - `JOBS` - parallel slots (default: `min(nproc, free_shm_MB / 256)`, capped pragmatically at 8)
  - `LOG_DIR` - where per-test logs land (default: `mktemp -d under /tmp`)
- **Outputs:** Per-test logs in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`, summary TSV at `$LOG_DIR/results.tsv`
- **Test Categories:**
  - **RT_TESTS (16):** `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_*` - run under both CSR and PC modes if `MODE=both`
  - **NEG_TESTS (4):** Error-condition drivers; run once
  - **DEMO_TESTS (7):** Switching-mode demos (T1-T7); run once
- **Exit Code:** 0 only if all tests pass; failed tests printed at end
- **Timeout:** Per-test override via `TIMEOUT=<secs>` in fixture env prefix

---

### 2. Test Harness (`tests/`)

**e2e.sh** (unified harness)
- **Location:** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/e2e.sh`
- **Required Env Vars:**
  - `FIXTURE` - basename of .elf in `tests/fixtures/` (no suffix)
  - `TRIGGER_MODE` - `csr|pc` (default: `csr`)
  - `EXPECT_RC` - exit code to assert (default: `0`)
  - `TIMEOUT` - vsim timeout in seconds (default: `60`)
  - `CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC` - if set, assert mcycle/minstret increased
- **Optional Env Vars:**
  - `VSIM_BIN`, `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_ELF`, `NM` - override auto-resolved paths
- **Three-Phase Flow:**
  1. **Phase 1/3:** Launch QEMU1 with plugin, drain entry state to `$STATE_QEMU`
  2. **Phase 2/3:** Launch vsim with `--resume-from-qemu`, `--handoff-out`, `--shared-mem-path`, `--shared-mem-size`; timeout enforced
  3. **Phase 3/3:** vsim spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub handback
- **Memory:** Shared 128 MB `/dev/shm` mmap created per-test; includes scratch region at `0x80FF0000` for V regfile transport
- **Outputs:** State files + final exit code

---

### 3. Fixtures (`tests/fixtures/Makefile`)

**Make Targets:**
- `all` (default) - Builds:
  - **Roundtrip asm (9):** `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_abi_aliases`, `rt_sign_bits`, `rt_pc_jump`, `rt_long_roi`, `rt_mem_loadstore`, `rt_unaligned_lh`, `rt_shared_mem`, `rt_csr_survival`
  - **C-level (4):** `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_printf`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_static_buf`
  - **V-enabled C (3):** `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`, `rt_c_v_matmul`
  - **Probe (1):** `probe_v_csr`
  - **Special:** `handoff_roundtrip`, `spin.elf`, `icount_spin.elf`, `handoff.elf`, `handoff_exit.elf`
- `clean` - Removes all .o and .elf files
- `demo` - Runs switching-mode demos (T1-T7)
- `dump` - objdump of legacy fixtures

**Compile Flags:**
- **Asm fixtures:** `-march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static`
- **C fixtures:** `-march=rv64gc -O1 -static -nostartfiles -nodefaultlibs`
- **V fixtures:** `-march=rv64gcv` (same as C but adds V ISA)
- **Linking:** C fixtures link `libgloss_vh.a` (semihosting via newlib)

**Env Var:**
- `TOOLCHAIN` - Toolchain root (defaults to `$(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)`); must have `bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc`, etc.

---

### 4. CMake Options (`verilator/CMakeLists.txt`)

**Invoked via:**
```sh
cd verilator && cmake -DOPTION=value -B build && cmake --build build
```

**Key Options:**
- `VSIM_DEBUG` (OFF) - Enable VCD waveform tracing
- `VSIM_DEBUG_FST` (OFF) - Enable FST (for long recordings)
- `VSIM_SAVABLE` (OFF) - Enable save/resume (slower)

- `VSIM_LINK_STATIC` (OFF) - Static linking
- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default: `config.yaml`)
- `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` - verilator path (auto-detected)
- `VSIM_CPUS` - Semicolon-separated list of CPUs to build (empty = all)
- `VSIM_CCS_OPT` - Extra optimization flags for CPU cluster subsystem

**Hybrid-Specific CMake (`verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`):**
- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (OFF) - Enables CTest registration for e2e fixtures
- `VSIM_QEMU_ROOT` - QEMU source tree root for plugin build
- Unit tests auto-registered (doctest-based); run via `ctest`

**Binary Output:**
- `verilator/build/sim_<cpu_variant>` (e.g., `sim_ax45mpv_premium`)

---

### 5. Plugin Build (`qemu_plugin/Makefile`)

**Usage:**
```sh
make QEMU_ROOT=/path/to/qemu
```

**Inputs:**
- `QEMU_ROOT` - QEMU source directory (required; must have `include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h`)
- `HYBRID_INCLUDE` - ABI header path (default: `../include`)
- `CC`, `CFLAGS` - Compiler overrides

**Outputs:**
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

**Sources:** `hybrid_handoff.c`, `bbv_emit.c`, shared ABI header

---

### 6. Documentation (`docs/`)

| File | Audience | Purpose |
|------|----------|---------|
| `USER_GUIDE.md` | End-user | Quick-start: build, configure, run one e2e test with worked example |
| `HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` | End-user + intern | Architecture, state categories, trigger modes, design rationale |
| `switching_modes_tutorial.md` | End-user | Per-mode drill-down (M1-M8) |
| `HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` | Developer | Internal state transport, tier-A/B CSRs, passthrough strategy |
| `simpoint_transport_comparison.md` | Developer | BBV + SimPoint I/O options |
| `vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` | Developer | Why vsim doesn't implement semihosting |
| `v_csr_plan.md` | Developer | V extension CSR restore roadmap |
| `TODO.md` | Developer | Known limitations and future work |
| `architecture.html` | Developer (legacy) | Graphviz diagram (use Mermaid in HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md instead) |

---

### Summary: All Developer Knobs

1. **`source ./setup.sh`** - Master builder (4-phase: config, qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint)
2. **`config.env`** - `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` path
3. **`MODE=csr|pc|both`** - Trigger mechanism
4. **`JOBS=N`** - Parallel test slots (default: auto-capped at 8)
5. **`TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`** - Per-fixture override
6. **`FILTER=regex`** - Test name subset
7. **`TIMEOUT=secs`** - vsim timeout per fixture
8. **`CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1`** - Strict cycle count validation
9. **`VSIM_DEBUG`, `VSIM_SAVABLE`** - CMake toggles for simulation features
10. **`VSIM_CONFIG_FILE`** - CPU YAML selection
11. **`-march=rv64gcv`** - V extension in fixtures (automatic for rt_c_v_* fixtures)

**For a unified main driver:** Hide knobs 2-8 behind sensible defaults; expose knobs 9-11 as `--debug-trace`, `--config cpu.yaml`, and infer V-ness from fixture name.

Excellent. Now I have enough to write my report. Let me compile findings:

## End-User Workflow Analysis: Andes Hybrid RISC-V Co-Simulator

### What ELF Programs Run Here?

Three fixture families live in `tests/fixtures/Makefile:20-36`:

**1. ASM-only round-trip fixtures** (`rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem`, `rt_pc_jump`, etc.)
- Minimal self-contained binaries testing GPRs, CSRs, memory surviva across handoffs
- No C runtime; pure assembly + `semihost_exit.inc` macro
- Compiled with `-march=rv64gc`, linked at 0x00100000 via `test.ld`

**2. C-level fixtures** (`rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_printf`)
- Full newlib+libgloss_vh (semihosting) C runtime; `crt0.S` + `trap.S` + `handler.c`
- MUST use shared `/dev/shm` mmap (128 MB) so stack/heap survive QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2
- Phase-2 rule (section 1 line 30): NO syscalls inside `enter_vsim()` ... `exit_vsim()` span because vsim has no semihosting
- Built with `-march=rv64gc`, linked via `rt_c.ld`, linked against `-lgloss_vh` + `-lc` + `-lgcc`

**3. V (RVV) fixtures** (`rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`, `rt_c_v_matmul`)
- C+RVV intrinsics; require `-march=rv64gcv`
- Ship all 32 V regs + V CSRs (vstart, vtype, vl, vcsr, vxsat, vxrm) through shared scratch region at 0x80FF0000
- Helper: `v_regfile_xfer.c` + `v_regfile_xfer.h` wraps the bulk VSE8/VLE8 bookkeeping
- Example: `rt_c_v_regs.c:1-60` shows FIR compute split across phases: QEMU1 (generate x via VID+1) -> vsim (filter) -> QEMU2 (verify)

**Hybrid-compatibility requirement:** Each fixture must mark handoff boundaries with either:
- **CSR mode (M1, default):** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (enter) + `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) — embedded in code or asm
- **PC mode (M2):** Export `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` global labels (resolved via `nm`)
- **icount mode (M3):** No markers; trigger on retired insn count instead

C fixtures use inline helpers `enter_vsim()`/`exit_vsim()` from `rt_c_helpers.h:34-45` which emit the CSR insns + a `"memory"` clobber to force compiler spill/reload of globals.

---

### Running One Fixture End-to-End

`tests/e2e.sh` is the unified harness spawning three processes. Flow (lines 71-137):

**Phase 1 (QEMU1):** 60-80 seconds  
```bash
VSIM_QEMU \
  -object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/<tmpfile>,size=128MB \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios $ELF \
  -plugin VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=/tmp/state_q1.bin
```
Drains at CSR trigger -> writes `hybrid_state_v1` (740 bytes, `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) -> exit code 200

**Phase 2 (vsim):** 5-30 seconds  
```bash
timeout 60 VSIM_BIN $ELF \
  --resume-from-qemu /tmp/state_q1.bin \
  --handoff-out /tmp/state_v.bin \
  --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/<tmpfile> \
  --qemu-binary VSIM_QEMU
```
Restores GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs via DMI, executes ROI, drains on exit trigger, spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub

**Phase 3 (QEMU2):** Spawned by vsim subprocess  
Connects via gdbstub, restores state via P-packets, runs to semihosting exit, final RC

**Success criterion:** all three phases complete with expected exit codes + no state corruption (mcycle/minstret strictly increasing). Logs go to `/tmp/e2e-<RAND>/` per-test.

---

### CPU Configs & Variants

`verilator/config.yaml:8-16` declares one active CPU:
- **ax45mpv_premium** (default, currently only one built)
- RTL package: `external/ax45mpv_premium/`
- Verilator threads: 8, unroll-count 256
- User can switch via `cmake -DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE=other.yaml` but only one CPU is pre-built

---

### Today's User Pain Points

**Friction 1: Multi-step setup before any run**
- Must `source ./setup.sh` (builds QEMU, vsim, plugin, SimPoint) — ~5 min cold start
- Must `make -C tests/fixtures -j` separately — ~30s
- Both mandatory; no single "build everything" shortcut that users think of first
- If you forget either, harness emits cryptic "skip: ... not built; run 'make' ..." error (USER_GUIDE.md:673)

**Friction 2: JOBS=8 cap is non-obvious**
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh` defaults to `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` = 128 on this 128-core host
- Running 128 vsim Verilator processes saturates CPU, causes timeouts; documented in CLAUDE.md + project memory
- No auto-downgrade or warning; users must know to set `JOBS=8` or watch suite timeout mysteriously
- USER_GUIDE.md:633-634 does mention it, but only after the "Running full test suite" section

**Friction 3: Fixture build is separate toolchain dance**
- `make -C tests/fixtures` requires `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/path` exported
- `config.env.example` → `config.env` (gitignore) workflow is hidden in setup.sh and USER_GUIDE.md:80-101
- No visual confirmation that fixtures built; users blindly hope `rt_c_v_regs.elf` exists
- Three fixture types (asm, C, V) have different link rules but no online UI to pick which to build

**Friction 4: No single-command "run my ELF"**
- To run a fixture: `FIXTURE=rt_c_hello TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 bash tests/e2e.sh`
- Or use a demo: `bash tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- But what if you have your OWN binary? You must either:
  - Write it in ASM with semihost_exit.inc markers
  - Wrap it in the C runtime (link `crt0.S`, trap.S, handler.c, libgloss_vh)
  - Insert `enter_vsim()` / `exit_vsim()` macros
  - Know which trigger mode (M1 CSR, M2 PC, M3 icount) matches your binary
  - No guided wizard or sanity check

**Friction 5: Trigger modes (M1..M8) are scattered**
- M1 (CSR): default, explained in USER_GUIDE.md:407
- M2 (PC): requires `_hybrid_enter_pc`/`_hybrid_exit_pc` symbols; USER_GUIDE.md:450
- M3 (icount): USER_GUIDE.md:492 but no fixtures supplied that use it standalone
- M4..M8 (SimPoint + archive replay): demos exist (`demo_simpoint_loop.sh`) but orchestrator (`python3 -m orchestrator.main`) is Python-based and opaque
- No quick "detect my binary's capability" helper

**Friction 6: Verbose output & error messages**
- Each harness run prints 4-5 lines per phase; test suite output is noisy
- Failures print full `$SIM_LOG` to stdout (tests/e2e.sh:132-133), which is 100+ lines of SystemC/Verilator debug

- Section 2: config surfaces (YAML, env vars, plugin args — all 4 entry points documented)

- No "which trigger mode for me" flowchart that doesn't require editing your binary

- Orchestrator (M6/M7) is documented in section 2.5 + 4.6 but requires hand-written YAML plan; no auto mode
- No end-to-end demo that doesn't require pre-built fixtures (all demos hardcode `rt_c_v_regs.elf`)

- F-T1.2: FP regs not restored to vsim (ResumeDriver gap)

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have enough to write my report. Let me compile findings:

## End-User Workflow Analysis: Andes Hybrid RISC-V Co-Simulator

### What ELF Programs Run Here?

Three fixture families live in `tests/fixtures/Makefile:20-36`:

**1. ASM-only round-trip fixtures** (`rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem`, `rt_pc_jump`, etc.)
- Minimal self-contained binaries testing GPRs, CSRs, memory surviva across handoffs
- No C runtime; pure assembly + `semihost_exit.inc` macro
- Compiled with `-march=rv64gc`, linked at 0x00100000 via `test.ld`

**2. C-level fixtures** (`rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_printf`)
- Full newlib+libgloss_vh (semihosting) C runtime; `crt0.S` + `trap.S` + `handler.c`
- MUST use shared `/dev/shm` mmap (128 MB) so stack/heap survive QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2
- Phase-2 rule (section 1 line 30): NO syscalls inside `enter_vsim()` ... `exit_vsim()` span because vsim has no semihosting
- Built with `-march=rv64gc`, linked via `rt_c.ld`, linked against `-lgloss_vh` + `-lc` + `-lgcc`

**3. V (RVV) fixtures** (`rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`, `rt_c_v_matmul`)
- C+RVV intrinsics; require `-march=rv64gcv`
- Ship all 32 V regs + V CSRs (vstart, vtype, vl, vcsr, vxsat, vxrm) through shared scratch region at 0x80FF0000
- Helper: `v_regfile_xfer.c` + `v_regfile_xfer.h` wraps the bulk VSE8/VLE8 bookkeeping
- Example: `rt_c_v_regs.c:1-60` shows FIR compute split across phases: QEMU1 (generate x via VID+1) -> vsim (filter) -> QEMU2 (verify)

**Hybrid-compatibility requirement:** Each fixture must mark handoff boundaries with either:
- **CSR mode (M1, default):** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (enter) + `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) — embedded in code or asm
- **PC mode (M2):** Export `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` global labels (resolved via `nm`)
- **icount mode (M3):** No markers; trigger on retired insn count instead

C fixtures use inline helpers `enter_vsim()`/`exit_vsim()` from `rt_c_helpers.h:34-45` which emit the CSR insns + a `"memory"` clobber to force compiler spill/reload of globals.

---

### Running One Fixture End-to-End

`tests/e2e.sh` is the unified harness spawning three processes. Flow (lines 71-137):

**Phase 1 (QEMU1):** 60-80 seconds  
```bash
VSIM_QEMU \
  -object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/<tmpfile>,size=128MB \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios $ELF \
  -plugin VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=/tmp/state_q1.bin
```
Drains at CSR trigger -> writes `hybrid_state_v1` (740 bytes, `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) -> exit code 200

**Phase 2 (vsim):** 5-30 seconds  
```bash
timeout 60 VSIM_BIN $ELF \
  --resume-from-qemu /tmp/state_q1.bin \
  --handoff-out /tmp/state_v.bin \
  --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/<tmpfile> \
  --qemu-binary VSIM_QEMU
```
Restores GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs via DMI, executes ROI, drains on exit trigger, spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub

**Phase 3 (QEMU2):** Spawned by vsim subprocess  
Connects via gdbstub, restores state via P-packets, runs to semihosting exit, final RC

**Success criterion:** all three phases complete with expected exit codes + no state corruption (mcycle/minstret strictly increasing). Logs go to `/tmp/e2e-<RAND>/` per-test.

---

### CPU Configs & Variants

`verilator/config.yaml:8-16` declares one active CPU:
- **ax45mpv_premium** (default, currently only one built)
- RTL package: `external/ax45mpv_premium/`
- Verilator threads: 8, unroll-count 256
- User can switch via `cmake -DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE=other.yaml` but only one CPU is pre-built

---

### Today's User Pain Points

**Friction 1: Multi-step setup before any run**
- Must `source ./setup.sh` (builds QEMU, vsim, plugin, SimPoint) — ~5 min cold start
- Must `make -C tests/fixtures -j` separately — ~30s
- Both mandatory; no single "build everything" shortcut that users think of first
- If you forget either, harness emits cryptic "skip: ... not built; run 'make' ..." error (USER_GUIDE.md:673)

**Friction 2: JOBS=8 cap is non-obvious**
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh` defaults to `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` = 128 on this 128-core host
- Running 128 vsim Verilator processes saturates CPU, causes timeouts; documented in CLAUDE.md + project memory
- No auto-downgrade or warning; users must know to set `JOBS=8` or watch suite timeout mysteriously
- USER_GUIDE.md:633-634 does mention it, but only after the "Running full test suite" section

**Friction 3: Fixture build is separate toolchain dance**
- `make -C tests/fixtures` requires `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/path` exported
- `config.env.example` → `config.env` (gitignore) workflow is hidden in setup.sh and USER_GUIDE.md:80-101
- No visual confirmation that fixtures built; users blindly hope `rt_c_v_regs.elf` exists
- Three fixture types (asm, C, V) have different link rules but no online UI to pick which to build

**Friction 4: No single-command "run my ELF"**
- To run a fixture: `FIXTURE=rt_c_hello TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 bash tests/e2e.sh`
- Or use a demo: `bash tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- But what if you have your OWN binary? You must either:
  - Write it in ASM with semihost_exit.inc markers
  - Wrap it in the C runtime (link `crt0.S`, trap.S, handler.c, libgloss_vh)
  - Insert `enter_vsim()` / `exit_vsim()` macros
  - Know which trigger mode (M1 CSR, M2 PC, M3 icount) matches your binary
  - No guided wizard or sanity check

**Friction 5: Trigger modes (M1..M8) are scattered**
- M1 (CSR): default, explained in USER_GUIDE.md:407
- M2 (PC): requires `_hybrid_enter_pc`/`_hybrid_exit_pc` symbols; USER_GUIDE.md:450
- M3 (icount): USER_GUIDE.md:492 but no fixtures supplied that use it standalone
- M4..M8 (SimPoint + archive replay): demos exist (`demo_simpoint_loop.sh`) but orchestrator (`python3 -m orchestrator.main`) is Python-based and opaque
- No quick "detect my binary's capability" helper

**Friction 6: Verbose output & error messages**
- Each harness run prints 4-5 lines per phase; test suite output is noisy
- Failures print full `$SIM_LOG` to stdout (tests/e2e.sh:132-133), which is 100+ lines of SystemC/Verilator debug
- No summary "X/Y fixtures passed" at end of `run_e2e.sh`; users must grep logs for `[FAIL]`

---

### What's Documented vs. What's Missing

**USER_GUIDE.md (comprehensive; 690 lines):**
- Section 1: build & env (clear; copy-pastable commands)
- Section 2: config surfaces (YAML, env vars, plugin args — all 4 entry points documented)
- Section 3: switching modes M1..M8 decision tree (good flowchart; USER_GUIDE.md:373)
- Section 4: walk-throughs for M1..M7 with verbatim CLI examples
- Section 5: test suite runner (`JOBS=8` warned but only here; USER_GUIDE.md:633)
- Section 6: failure table (helps debug)
- Section 7: pointers to deep dives (HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md, switching_modes_tutorial.md)

**Missing / unclear:**
- No "quickstart for your own binary" guide — how do you port a random RISC-V ELF to the hybrid sim?
- No "which trigger mode for me" flowchart that doesn't require editing your binary
- No validation tool ("check if my binary is hybrid-compatible")
- Orchestrator (M6/M7) is documented in section 2.5 + 4.6 but requires hand-written YAML plan; no auto mode
- No end-to-end demo that doesn't require pre-built fixtures (all demos hardcode `rt_c_v_regs.elf`)

**TODO.md (50+ failure modes tracked):**
- F-T1.1: semihosting inside ROI hangs infinitely (no vsim semihost handler)
- F-T1.2: FP regs not restored to vsim (ResumeDriver gap)
- Documented but not yet fixed; limits real workload support

---

### Summary: What an End User Actually Wants

```
I have a RISC-V ELF binary (mine, or someone else's).
I want to:
  1. Run it fast on QEMU (MHz-level) until some trigger
  2. Switch to vsim (cycle-accurate) for a critical region
  3. Continue on QEMU (fast again) to completion
  4. Check that results are correct

I don't want to:
  - Remember JOBS=8 flag
  - Know how CSR vs. PC vs. icount triggers work
  - Write a fixture from scratch
  - Manually specify three processes (QEMU1, vsim, QEMU2)
  - Read docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md for your first run
```

**The missing UI:** A single command like:
```bash
./sim run my_app.elf --roi-enter 0x10234 --roi-exit 0x10456
```
That auto-detects if the binary has `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` symbols, auto-picks a trigger mode, auto-builds shared mmap, spawns the three-phase pipeline, and prints "PASS" / "FAIL + logs" at the end. The demos hint at this (demo_csr_oneshot.sh wraps e2e.sh) but there's no single entry point that hides the `FIXTURE=... TRIGGER_MODE=... EXPECT_RC=... bash tests/e2e.sh` plumbing.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
#
# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
# fine with it.
#
# Env:
#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under tests/fixtures/ (no .elf suffix).
#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

hybrid_resolve_paths

NM="${NM:-${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:+$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}}"
NM="${NM:-riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT"  | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim uses the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-e2e-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state"
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Driver: build qemu + vsim + plugin + simpoint, then add build dirs to PATH.
3	# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
4	#   source ./setup.sh                       # build all
5	#   source ./setup.sh clean [target]        # clean qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all (default: all)
6	
7	if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
8	  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
9	elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
10	  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
11	else
12	  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
13	fi
14	
15	# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
16	# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
17	# under nounset).
18	_hybrid_setup_main() {
19	  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
20	    emulate -L sh
21	    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
22	  else
23	    local -
24	    set -euo pipefail
25	  fi
26	
27	  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_DIR QEMU_SRC PLUGIN_SO SIMPOINT_SRC SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH CONFIG_FILE CONFIG_TEMPLATE
28	  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
29	  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
30	  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/verilator/build"
31	  PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/qemu_plugin"
32	  QEMU_SRC="$ROOT/QEMU"
33	  PLUGIN_SO="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
34	  SIMPOINT_SRC="$ROOT/tools/simpoint"
35	  SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH="$SIMPOINT_SRC/bin/simpoint"
36	  CONFIG_FILE="$ROOT/config.env"
37	  CONFIG_TEMPLATE="$ROOT/config.env.example"
38	
39	  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
40	  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
41	  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
42	
43	  clean_qemu()   { step "cleaning qemu build ($QEMU_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$QEMU_BIN_DIR"; ok "qemu cleaned"; }
44	  clean_vsim()   { step "cleaning vsim build ($VSIM_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$VSIM_BIN_DIR"; ok "vsim cleaned"; }
45	  clean_plugin() {
46	    step "cleaning plugin build ($PLUGIN_DIR)"
47	    if [ -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
48	      make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" clean
49	    else
50	      rm -f "$PLUGIN_SO" "$PLUGIN_DIR"/*.o
51	    fi
52	    ok "plugin cleaned"
53	  }
54	  clean_simpoint() {
55	    step "cleaning simpoint build ($SIMPOINT_SRC)"
56	    if [ -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
57	      make -C "$SIMPOINT_SRC" clean
58	    else
59	      rm -f "$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
60	    fi
61	    ok "simpoint cleaned"
62	  }
63	
64	  if [ "${1:-}" = "clean" ]; then
65	    case "${2:-all}" in
66	      qemu)     clean_qemu ;;
67	      vsim)     clean_vsim ;;
68	      plugin)   clean_plugin ;;
69	      simpoint) clean_simpoint ;;
70	      all)      clean_qemu; clean_vsim; clean_plugin; clean_simpoint ;;
71	      *)        err "usage: source $_SETUP_SRC clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]"; return 1 ;;
72	    esac
73	    return 0
74	  fi
75	
76	  step "0/4 loading per-host config"
77	  if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
78	    if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_TEMPLATE" ]; then
79	      err "missing $CONFIG_TEMPLATE; cannot create $CONFIG_FILE"
80	      return 1
81	    fi
82	    cp "$CONFIG_TEMPLATE" "$CONFIG_FILE"
83	    ok "created $CONFIG_FILE from template -- edit it if your toolchain lives elsewhere"
84	  fi
85	  # shellcheck source=/dev/null
86	  . "$CONFIG_FILE"
87	  if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ]; then
88	    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN not set; check $CONFIG_FILE"
89	    return 1
90	  fi
91	  if [ ! -d "$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin" ]; then
92	    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN has no bin/ directory; edit $CONFIG_FILE"
93	    return 1
94	  fi
95	  export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
96	  ok "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN"
97	
98	  step "1/4 building qemu"
99	  "$ROOT/scripts/build_qemu.sh"
100	  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
101	
102	  step "2/4 building vsim"
103	  "$ROOT/scripts/build_vsim.sh"
104	  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"
105	
106	  step "3/4 building hybrid plugin"
107	  if [ ! -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
108	    err "missing $PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile"
109	    return 1
110	  fi
111	  if [ ! -f "$QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h" ]; then
112	    err "missing $QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h - QEMU submodule not initialized"
113	    return 1
114	  fi
115	  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_SRC"
116	  ok "plugin -> $PLUGIN_SO"
117	
118	  step "4/4 building simpoint clustering binary"
119	  if [ ! -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
120	    err "missing $SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile - tools/simpoint submodule not initialized"
121	    return 1
122	  fi
123	  "$ROOT/scripts/build_simpoint.sh"
124	  ok "simpoint -> $SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
125	
126	  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
127	  case ":$PATH:" in
128	    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
129	    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
130	  esac
131	  case ":$PATH:" in
132	    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
133	    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
134	  esac
135	  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"
136	
137	  step "env: exporting VSIM_QEMU / VSIM_PLUGIN / SIMPOINT_BIN for e2e scripts"
138	  export VSIM_QEMU="$QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
139	  export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_SO"
140	  export SIMPOINT_BIN="$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
141	  ok "VSIM_QEMU=$VSIM_QEMU"
142	  ok "VSIM_PLUGIN=$VSIM_PLUGIN"
143	  ok "SIMPOINT_BIN=$SIMPOINT_BIN"
144	
145	  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
146	    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
147	    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
148	  fi
149	}
150	
151	_hybrid_setup_main "$@"
152	unset -f _hybrid_setup_main 2>/dev/null
153	unset _SETUP_SRC
154

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim + plugin + simpoint, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh                       # build all
#   source ./setup.sh clean [target]        # clean qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all (default: all)

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_DIR QEMU_SRC PLUGIN_SO SIMPOINT_SRC SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH CONFIG_FILE CONFIG_TEMPLATE
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/verilator/build"
  PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/qemu_plugin"
  QEMU_SRC="$ROOT/QEMU"
  PLUGIN_SO="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  SIMPOINT_SRC="$ROOT/tools/simpoint"
  SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH="$SIMPOINT_SRC/bin/simpoint"
  CONFIG_FILE="$ROOT/config.env"
  CONFIG_TEMPLATE="$ROOT/config.env.example"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  clean_qemu()   { step "cleaning qemu build ($QEMU_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$QEMU_BIN_DIR"; ok "qemu cleaned"; }
  clean_vsim()   { step "cleaning vsim build ($VSIM_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$VSIM_BIN_DIR"; ok "vsim cleaned"; }
  clean_plugin() {
    step "cleaning plugin build ($PLUGIN_DIR)"
    if [ -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" clean
    else
      rm -f "$PLUGIN_SO" "$PLUGIN_DIR"/*.o
    fi
    ok "plugin cleaned"
  }
  clean_simpoint() {
    step "cleaning simpoint build ($SIMPOINT_SRC)"
    if [ -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$SIMPOINT_SRC" clean
    else
      rm -f "$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
    fi
    ok "simpoint cleaned"
  }

  if [ "${1:-}" = "clean" ]; then
    case "${2:-all}" in
      qemu)     clean_qemu ;;
      vsim)     clean_vsim ;;
      plugin)   clean_plugin ;;
      simpoint) clean_simpoint ;;
      all)      clean_qemu; clean_vsim; clean_plugin; clean_simpoint ;;
      *)        err "usage: source $_SETUP_SRC clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]"; return 1 ;;
    esac
    return 0
  fi

  step "0/4 loading per-host config"
  if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_TEMPLATE" ]; then
      err "missing $CONFIG_TEMPLATE; cannot create $CONFIG_FILE"
      return 1
    fi
    cp "$CONFIG_TEMPLATE" "$CONFIG_FILE"
    ok "created $CONFIG_FILE from template -- edit it if your toolchain lives elsewhere"
  fi
  # shellcheck source=/dev/null
  . "$CONFIG_FILE"
  if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ]; then
    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN not set; check $CONFIG_FILE"
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ! -d "$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin" ]; then
    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN has no bin/ directory; edit $CONFIG_FILE"
    return 1
  fi
  export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
  ok "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN"

  step "1/4 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/scripts/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/4 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/scripts/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "3/4 building hybrid plugin"
  if [ ! -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile"
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h" ]; then
    err "missing $QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h - QEMU submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_SRC"
  ok "plugin -> $PLUGIN_SO"

  step "4/4 building simpoint clustering binary"
  if [ ! -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile - tools/simpoint submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  "$ROOT/scripts/build_simpoint.sh"
  ok "simpoint -> $SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  step "env: exporting VSIM_QEMU / VSIM_PLUGIN / SIMPOINT_BIN for e2e scripts"
  export VSIM_QEMU="$QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
  export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_SO"
  export SIMPOINT_BIN="$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
  ok "VSIM_QEMU=$VSIM_QEMU"
  ok "VSIM_PLUGIN=$VSIM_PLUGIN"
  ok "SIMPOINT_BIN=$SIMPOINT_BIN"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main "$@"
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main 2>/dev/null
unset _SETUP_SRC

1	# TODO: Failure Modes Backlog
2	
3	Prioritized list of conditions under which the hybrid QEMU+vsim simulator
4	will silently fail or hang on real workloads. Derived from a 2026-05-17
5	audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `verilator/src/hybrid/`,
6	`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
7	`verilator/tests/hybrid/`.
8	
9	Format: one row per failure mode.
10	
11	- Trigger: the production scenario that exposes the bug
12	- Breaks: the observable symptom
13	- Source: file(s) where the gap lives
14	- Fix: the most feasible proposed solution
15	- Status: not-started | in-progress | done
16	
17	## Tier 1 - Will fail on the next real workload
18	
19	### F-T1.1 Semihosting inside ROI
20	
21	- Trigger: Any C fixture calling `printf`, `malloc`, `_exit`, or any
22	  libgloss-routed syscall between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()`.
23	- Breaks: libgloss_vh emits the semihosting magic triple
24	  (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`); vsim's trap handler
25	  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
26	  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
27	- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
28	  `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
29	- Fix: Add a host-side semihosting handler inside vsim so libgloss_vh
30	  keeps working unchanged across the ROI. Pre-scan the ELF for the
31	  magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` (same pattern
32	  template as `csr_trigger.hpp:37`); map each ebreak PC to a new
33	  `HYBRID_SEMIHOST_CALL` trigger kind. HandoffController dispatches to
34	  a `SemihostHandler` that: halts the hart via DMI, reads `a0` (call
35	  number) + `a1` (arg block ptr) using the existing
36	  `read_64bit_register` helper, services SYS_WRITE/SYS_WRITE0/
37	  SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED on the host (memory reads via the shared mmap
38	  already mapped by orchestrator), writes the return value back into
39	  `a0`, advances DPC by 12 bytes (past the full triple, not just
40	  ebreak), then resumes. No fixture / libgloss changes needed.
41	- Status: not-started
42	
43	### F-T1.2 FP state not restored to vsim
44	
45	- Trigger: Any workload using f0..f31 inside ROI.
46	- Breaks: QEMU1 plugin drains FPRs into `state.bin`. ResumeDriver does
47	  not write FPRs. vsim FPRs remain at RTL reset value. StateDrain
48	  passes through the stale QEMU1 snapshot to QEMU2 unchanged.
49	  FP computations in ROI silently corrupt register contents.
50	- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
51	  `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
52	- Fix: Shipped in commit `59be1af` (2026-05-17). ResumeDriver writes
53	  f0..f31 via DM Abstract Commands after gating mstatus.FS;
54	  StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough freshly reads f0..f31 when
55	  HYBRID_FLAG_FP is set.
56	- Status: done
57	
58	### F-T1.3 PMP state not restored to vsim
59	
60	- Trigger: Any workload depending on PMP-enforced memory regions
61	  inside ROI.
62	- Breaks: Plugin drains pmpcfg[0..15] and pmpaddr[0..63] but
63	  ResumeDriver writes none. vsim sees zeroed PMP entries; any
64	  write-protect or execute-lock setup from QEMU1 evaporates.
65	- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
66	- Fix: Mirror the FP pattern from F-T1.2. ResumeDriver writes
67	  pmpaddr[0..63] (CSRs 0x3B0..0x3EF) first, then pmpcfg[0..15] (CSRs
68	  0x3A0..0x3AF) via `write_csr_abstract_or_pb`; addr-before-cfg so
69	  lock bits apply last. StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough adds the
70	  mirror read loop. ABI fields already exist; no version bump.
71	- Status: not-started
72	
73	### F-T1.4 Exception state lost across vsim ROI
74	
75	- Trigger: Any trap (illegal-insn, page-fault, alignment, ebreak) fires
76	  inside ROI.
77	- Breaks: StateDrain returns the QEMU1 snapshot for mcause/mtval/mepc
78	  instead of vsim's current trap state. QEMU2 receives stale values;
79	  any post-handoff trap diagnostic logic is wrong.
80	- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (passthrough path)
81	- Fix: In StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough, always freshly read
82	  mcause (0x342), mtval (0x343), mepc (0x341), and mstatus (0x300)
83	  via the existing read_64bit_register helper. Overwrite the
84	  pass-through values unconditionally; no flag gate needed.
85	- Status: not-started
86	
87	### F-T1.5 LR/SC reservation not transferred
88	
89	- Trigger: An lr.{w,d} retires before the trigger and the matching
90	  sc.{w,d} retires after - or any atomic across the handoff.
91	- Breaks: Reservation is not in the ABI struct, never round-trips.
92	  sc.* always fails on re-entry, so any `__atomic_compare_exchange`
93	  loop spins forever.
94	- Source: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (missing field); resume/drain
95	  both
96	- Fix: Enforce trigger discipline rather than round-tripping the
97	  reservation. QEMU plugin checks `env->load_res.valid` at drain time
98	  and aborts with a clear diagnostic if a reservation is pending,
99	  asking the workload to place the trigger after a `fence rw,rw` or
100	  outside any lr/sc-bracketed region. Matches the existing "trigger
101	  is a sync point" assumption; no ABI bump.
102	- Status: not-started
103	
104	## Tier 2 - Will fail when scaling
105	
106	### F-T2.1 priv mode hardcoded to M
107	
108	- Trigger: Linux/kernel workload running in S- or U-mode at the
109	  trigger boundary.
110	- Breaks: `state_drain.hpp:40` and `resume_driver.hpp:40` force
111	  `priv=3`; DCSR.prv never written. `dret` always resumes in M-mode.
112	  S/U code raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
113	- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
114	  `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
115	- Fix: Drain - replace `state.priv = 3` with a DCSR read (CSR 0x7B0,
116	  mask bits 1:0). Resume - before `request_resume()`, write DCSR.prv
117	  from state.priv via `write_csr_abstract_or_pb` so the subsequent
118	  `dret` restores the recorded mode. ABI `priv` field already exists.
119	- Status: not-started
120	
121	### F-T2.2 Workload exceeding 128MB mmap
122	
123	- Trigger: Heap, stack, or static data extending past the default 128MB
124	  shared mmap.
125	- Breaks: vsim's SimpleMemory bounds reject the access or silently
126	  truncate; QEMU sees MMIO instead of RAM. No startup-time bounds
127	  check on ELF segments against mmap size.
128	- Source: `verilator/src/platform/simple_memory.hpp`,
129	  orchestrator mmap sizing
130	- Fix: Orchestrator startup parses the ELF PHDRs; sums max(vaddr +
131	  memsz) across LOAD segments; aborts with a diagnostic if the value
132	  exceeds the mmap size. Expose mmap size via env var
133	  `HYBRID_MMAP_BYTES` (default 128MB) so large workloads opt in.
134	- Status: not-started
135	
136	### F-T2.3 Multi-hart resume path
137	
138	- Trigger: `_NDS_NHART=2` configuration; workload using hart 1, atomic
139	  fences, IPIs, or hart-to-hart sync.
140	- Breaks: ABI carries `hart_id` and per-hart arrays but ResumeDriver
141	  and StateDrain only handle hart 0. Resuming hart 1 from hart 0's
142	  state corrupts both.
143	- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/{resume_driver,state_drain}.hpp`,
144	  `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (thread selection)
145	- Fix: Wrap ResumeDriver/StateDrain in a per-hart loop. Before each
146	  pass, write DMCONTROL.hartsello (bits 16:25) to select the hart;
147	  re-poll allhalted, then run the existing single-hart code.
148	  qemu_handback prefixes each per-hart P-packet block with `Hg
149	  <thread-id>`. Gated by hart_count > 1 so single-hart path is
150	  unchanged.
151	- Status: not-started
152	
153	### F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret monotonicity
154	
155	- Trigger: Long-running workload; or any consumer reading these CSRs
156	  across the handoff.
157	- Breaks: No test asserts monotonicity. If the icount trigger is off
158	  by one, vsim's mcycle could be less than QEMU1's exit mcycle and
159	  nothing would catch it.
160	- Source: gap in test suite; ABI does carry mcycle/minstret
161	- Fix: Shipped in commit `a289fea` (2026-05-17). ResumeDriver writes
162	  mcycle (CSR 0xB00) and minstret (0xB02) into the vsim hart so cycle
163	  counts continue from QEMU1's drain. StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough
164	  freshly reads both unconditionally so the vsim phase's contribution
165	  survives into QEMU2. e2e.sh + CTest entry
166	  test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity (handoff_roundtrip) assert strict
167	  monotonicity on the drain files (mcycle@624, minstret@632).
168	- Status: done
169	
170	### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI
171	
172	- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
173	  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
174	- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
175	  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
176	  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
177	  vsim's view.
178	- Source: `verilator/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
179	  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
180	- Fix: Define a single allowlist of RAM + permitted-MMIO ranges in
181	  `src/platform/interconnect.hpp`. SimpleMemory::read/write checks
182	  every access against the allowlist; out-of-range aborts with a
183	  diagnostic logging the offending PC + address. Implements the
184	  Phase-5 interconnect watch.
185	- Status: not-started
186	
187	## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite
188	
189	### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos
190	
191	- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
192	  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
193	  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
194	- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
195	  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
196	  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
197	  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
198	- Source: `verilator/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
199	- Fix: In each demo, after QEMU2 connects via gdbstub, issue a single
200	  `g` packet to read the full register file; parse and memcmp byte
201	  by byte against the corresponding fields in state.bin. Fail the
202	  demo on mismatch. ~30 lines of bash per demo via a shared helper.
203	- Status: not-started
204	
205	### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations
206	
207	- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
208	  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
209	  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
210	- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
211	  hit in production.
212	- Source: gap in `scripts/run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
213	- Fix: Parameterize `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with separate `ENTRY_MODE` and
214	  `EXIT_MODE` env vars (default = MODE if unset). Add a top-level
215	  `matrix` mode that iterates the cross-product across one canonical
216	  fixture, skipping pairings the plugin rejects up front.
217	- Status: not-started
218	
219	### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift
220	
221	- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
222	  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
223	- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
224	  with no CI gate.
225	- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
226	- Fix: Add `scripts/check_upstream_drift.sh` that, for each submodule,
227	  `git fetch`es the upstream remote and reports commits since the
228	  pinned SHA. Schedule a weekly CI job that runs the script and
229	  attaches the report to an auto-filed GitHub issue when non-empty.
230	- Status: not-started
231	
232	### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent
233	
234	- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
235	  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
236	- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
237	  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
238	  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
239	- Source: gap in `verilator/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
240	  validator
241	- Fix: At handoff entry, before any V transport, ResumeDriver reads
242	  the hart's `vlenb` CSR (0xC22) via Abstract Command and compares
243	  against `state.vlenb`. Mismatch aborts with a clear error naming
244	  both values. Same check on qemu_handback ingress side.
245	- Status: not-started
246	
247	### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes
248	
249	- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
250	  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
251	- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
252	  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
253	  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
254	  unguarded.
255	- Source: `verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
256	- Fix: Add three negative drivers: `rt_fault_partial_drain.sh`
257	  (truncate state.bin to half its size mid-handoff),
258	  `rt_fault_disk_full.sh` (run on a size-capped tmpfs smaller than
259	  state size), `rt_fault_mmap_resize.sh` (truncate the shared mmap
260	  while vsim is mid-execution). Each asserts a clean diagnostic exit.
261	- Status: not-started
262	
263	### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs
264	
265	- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
266	- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
267	  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
268	  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
269	- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
270	  bytes
271	- Fix: Add an e2e test that runs the same fixture twice and asserts
272	  `sha256sum state1.bin == sha256sum state2.bin`. Audit drain path:
273	  zero-initialise `reserved[]` explicitly in the plugin and in
274	  StateDrain; serialise CSR writes in canonical CSR-number order.
275	- Status: not-started
276	
277	### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code
278	
279	- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
280	- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
281	  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
282	  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
283	- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
284	  not detected
285	- Fix: At orchestrator startup, parse the ELF for `.text` PHDRs and
286	  call `mprotect(..., PROT_READ)` on the corresponding mmap pages.
287	  Any write fault becomes a clean SIGSEGV with a diagnostic naming
288	  the offending PC. SMC stays out-of-scope but is now detected
289	  instead of silently corrupting traces.
290	- Status: not-started
291	
292	### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2
293	
294	- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
295	  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
296	- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
297	  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
298	  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
299	- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
300	- Fix: In `connect_loopback`, install a `setitimer`-based watchdog
301	  before the gdb handshake. On SIGALRM, `kill(child_pid, SIGKILL)`
302	  then `waitpid(child_pid, ..., 0)` to reap, then throw. RAII guard
303	  ensures reap happens on any throw path.
304	- Status: not-started
305	
306	## Highest-leverage next moves
307	
308	1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
309	   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
310	   against the drained `state.bin`.
311	2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
312	   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
313	   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
314	3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
315	   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
316	   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.
317
