# TODO: Failure Modes Backlog

Prioritized list of conditions under which the hybrid QEMU+vsim simulator
will silently fail or hang on real workloads. Derived from a 2026-05-17
audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `verilator/src/hybrid/`,
`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
`verilator/tests/hybrid/`.

Format: one row per failure mode.

- Trigger: the production scenario that exposes the bug
- Breaks: the observable symptom
- Source: file(s) where the gap lives
- Fix: the most feasible proposed solution
- Status: not-started | in-progress | done

## Tier 1 - Will fail on the next real workload

### F-T1.1 Semihosting inside ROI

- Trigger: Any C fixture calling `printf`, `malloc`, `_exit`, or any
  libgloss-routed syscall between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()`.
- Breaks: libgloss_vh emits the semihosting magic triple
  (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`); vsim's trap handler
  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
  `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
- Fix: Add a host-side semihosting handler inside vsim so libgloss_vh
  keeps working unchanged across the ROI. Pre-scan the ELF for the
  magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` (same pattern
  template as `csr_trigger.hpp:37`); map each ebreak PC to a new
  `HYBRID_SEMIHOST_CALL` trigger kind. HandoffController dispatches to
  a `SemihostHandler` that: halts the hart via DMI, reads `a0` (call
  number) + `a1` (arg block ptr) using the existing
  `read_64bit_register` helper, services SYS_WRITE/SYS_WRITE0/
  SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED on the host (memory reads via the shared mmap
  already mapped by orchestrator), writes the return value back into
  `a0`, advances DPC by 12 bytes (past the full triple, not just
  ebreak), then resumes. No fixture / libgloss changes needed.
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.2 FP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload using f0..f31 inside ROI.
- Breaks: QEMU1 plugin drains FPRs into `state.bin`. ResumeDriver does
  not write FPRs. vsim FPRs remain at RTL reset value. StateDrain
  passes through the stale QEMU1 snapshot to QEMU2 unchanged.
  FP computations in ROI silently corrupt register contents.
- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Fix: Shipped in commit `59be1af` (2026-05-17). ResumeDriver writes
  f0..f31 via DM Abstract Commands after gating mstatus.FS;
  StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough freshly reads f0..f31 when
  HYBRID_FLAG_FP is set.
- Status: done

### F-T1.3 PMP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload depending on PMP-enforced memory regions
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: Plugin drains pmpcfg[0..15] and pmpaddr[0..63] but
  ResumeDriver writes none. vsim sees zeroed PMP entries; any
  write-protect or execute-lock setup from QEMU1 evaporates.
- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
- Fix: Mirror the FP pattern from F-T1.2. ResumeDriver writes
  pmpaddr[0..63] (CSRs 0x3B0..0x3EF) first, then pmpcfg[0..15] (CSRs
  0x3A0..0x3AF) via `write_csr_abstract_or_pb`; addr-before-cfg so
  lock bits apply last. StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough adds the
  mirror read loop. ABI fields already exist; no version bump.
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.4 Exception state lost across vsim ROI

- Trigger: Any trap (illegal-insn, page-fault, alignment, ebreak) fires
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: StateDrain returns the QEMU1 snapshot for mcause/mtval/mepc
  instead of vsim's current trap state. QEMU2 receives stale values;
  any post-handoff trap diagnostic logic is wrong.
- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (passthrough path)
- Fix: In StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough, always freshly read
  mcause (0x342), mtval (0x343), mepc (0x341), and mstatus (0x300)
  via the existing read_64bit_register helper. Overwrite the
  pass-through values unconditionally; no flag gate needed.
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.5 LR/SC reservation not transferred

- Trigger: An lr.{w,d} retires before the trigger and the matching
  sc.{w,d} retires after - or any atomic across the handoff.
- Breaks: Reservation is not in the ABI struct, never round-trips.
  sc.* always fails on re-entry, so any `__atomic_compare_exchange`
  loop spins forever.
- Source: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (missing field); resume/drain
  both
- Fix: Enforce trigger discipline rather than round-tripping the
  reservation. QEMU plugin checks `env->load_res.valid` at drain time
  and aborts with a clear diagnostic if a reservation is pending,
  asking the workload to place the trigger after a `fence rw,rw` or
  outside any lr/sc-bracketed region. Matches the existing "trigger
  is a sync point" assumption; no ABI bump.
- Status: not-started

## Tier 2 - Will fail when scaling

### F-T2.1 priv mode hardcoded to M

- Trigger: Linux/kernel workload running in S- or U-mode at the
  trigger boundary.
- Breaks: `state_drain.hpp:40` and `resume_driver.hpp:40` force
  `priv=3`; DCSR.prv never written. `dret` always resumes in M-mode.
  S/U code raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Fix: Drain - replace `state.priv = 3` with a DCSR read (CSR 0x7B0,
  mask bits 1:0). Resume - before `request_resume()`, write DCSR.prv
  from state.priv via `write_csr_abstract_or_pb` so the subsequent
  `dret` restores the recorded mode. ABI `priv` field already exists.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.2 Workload exceeding 128MB mmap

- Trigger: Heap, stack, or static data extending past the default 128MB
  shared mmap.
- Breaks: vsim's SimpleMemory bounds reject the access or silently
  truncate; QEMU sees MMIO instead of RAM. No startup-time bounds
  check on ELF segments against mmap size.
- Source: `verilator/src/platform/simple_memory.hpp`,
  orchestrator mmap sizing
- Fix: Orchestrator startup parses the ELF PHDRs; sums max(vaddr +
  memsz) across LOAD segments; aborts with a diagnostic if the value
  exceeds the mmap size. Expose mmap size via env var
  `HYBRID_MMAP_BYTES` (default 128MB) so large workloads opt in.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.3 Multi-hart resume path

- Trigger: `_NDS_NHART=2` configuration; workload using hart 1, atomic
  fences, IPIs, or hart-to-hart sync.
- Breaks: ABI carries `hart_id` and per-hart arrays but ResumeDriver
  and StateDrain only handle hart 0. Resuming hart 1 from hart 0's
  state corrupts both.
- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/{resume_driver,state_drain}.hpp`,
  `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (thread selection)
- Fix: Wrap ResumeDriver/StateDrain in a per-hart loop. Before each
  pass, write DMCONTROL.hartsello (bits 16:25) to select the hart;
  re-poll allhalted, then run the existing single-hart code.
  qemu_handback prefixes each per-hart P-packet block with `Hg
  <thread-id>`. Gated by hart_count > 1 so single-hart path is
  unchanged.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret monotonicity

- Trigger: Long-running workload; or any consumer reading these CSRs
  across the handoff.
- Breaks: No test asserts monotonicity. If the icount trigger is off
  by one, vsim's mcycle could be less than QEMU1's exit mcycle and
  nothing would catch it.
- Source: gap in test suite; ABI does carry mcycle/minstret
- Fix: Shipped in commit `a289fea` (2026-05-17). ResumeDriver writes
  mcycle (CSR 0xB00) and minstret (0xB02) into the vsim hart so cycle
  counts continue from QEMU1's drain. StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough
  freshly reads both unconditionally so the vsim phase's contribution
  survives into QEMU2. e2e.sh + CTest entry
  test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity (handoff_roundtrip) assert strict
  monotonicity on the drain files (mcycle@624, minstret@632).
- Status: done

### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI

- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
  vsim's view.
- Source: `verilator/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
- Fix: Define a single allowlist of RAM + permitted-MMIO ranges in
  `src/platform/interconnect.hpp`. SimpleMemory::read/write checks
  every access against the allowlist; out-of-range aborts with a
  diagnostic logging the offending PC + address. Implements the
  Phase-5 interconnect watch.
- Status: not-started

## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite

### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos

- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
- Source: `verilator/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
- Fix: In each demo, after QEMU2 connects via gdbstub, issue a single
  `g` packet to read the full register file; parse and memcmp byte
  by byte against the corresponding fields in state.bin. Fail the
  demo on mismatch. ~30 lines of bash per demo via a shared helper.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations

- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
  hit in production.
- Source: gap in `scripts/run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
- Fix: Parameterize `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with separate `ENTRY_MODE` and
  `EXIT_MODE` env vars (default = MODE if unset). Add a top-level
  `matrix` mode that iterates the cross-product across one canonical
  fixture, skipping pairings the plugin rejects up front.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift

- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
  with no CI gate.
- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
- Fix: Add `scripts/check_upstream_drift.sh` that, for each submodule,
  `git fetch`es the upstream remote and reports commits since the
  pinned SHA. Schedule a weekly CI job that runs the script and
  attaches the report to an auto-filed GitHub issue when non-empty.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent

- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
- Source: gap in `verilator/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
  validator
- Fix: At handoff entry, before any V transport, ResumeDriver reads
  the hart's `vlenb` CSR (0xC22) via Abstract Command and compares
  against `state.vlenb`. Mismatch aborts with a clear error naming
  both values. Same check on qemu_handback ingress side.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes

- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
  unguarded.
- Source: `verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
- Fix: Add three negative drivers: `rt_fault_partial_drain.sh`
  (truncate state.bin to half its size mid-handoff),
  `rt_fault_disk_full.sh` (run on a size-capped tmpfs smaller than
  state size), `rt_fault_mmap_resize.sh` (truncate the shared mmap
  while vsim is mid-execution). Each asserts a clean diagnostic exit.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs

- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
  bytes
- Fix: Add an e2e test that runs the same fixture twice and asserts
  `sha256sum state1.bin == sha256sum state2.bin`. Audit drain path:
  zero-initialise `reserved[]` explicitly in the plugin and in
  StateDrain; serialise CSR writes in canonical CSR-number order.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code

- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
  not detected
- Fix: At orchestrator startup, parse the ELF for `.text` PHDRs and
  call `mprotect(..., PROT_READ)` on the corresponding mmap pages.
  Any write fault becomes a clean SIGSEGV with a diagnostic naming
  the offending PC. SMC stays out-of-scope but is now detected
  instead of silently corrupting traces.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2

- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
- Source: `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
- Fix: In `connect_loopback`, install a `setitimer`-based watchdog
  before the gdb handshake. On SIGALRM, `kill(child_pid, SIGKILL)`
  then `waitpid(child_pid, ..., 0)` to reap, then throw. RAII guard
  ensures reap happens on any throw path.
- Status: not-started

## Highest-leverage next moves

1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
   against the drained `state.bin`.
2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.

# Hybrid Simulator User Guide

Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
not study its internals. After this guide you can:

1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
   with a concrete worked example.

For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.

---

## 0. The one-paragraph mental model

QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
hands control between them. One run is three legs:

```
   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
```

The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
mode".

hands control between them. One run is three legs:

```
   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
```

The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
mode".

content.content:
# Shared path resolution for the top-level e2e harness + drivers.
# Sourced (not executed). Provides:
#   TOP_ROOT                  - absolute path of the repo root
#   hybrid_resolve_paths()    - sets VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN,
#                               VSIM_ELF from TOP_ROOT (unless already
#                               exported by caller) and exits 77 (CTest
#                               skip) if any artifact is missing. Reads
#                               FIXTURE for the VSIM_ELF default.

TESTS_LIB_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$TESTS_LIB_DIR/../.." && pwd)

if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -f "$TOP_ROOT/config.env" ]; then
  . "$TOP_ROOT/config.env"
fi

hybrid_resolve_paths() {
  VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
  VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
  VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
  VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$TOP_ROOT/tests/fixtures/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
  for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
    if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
      echo "skip: $f not built; run 'source ./setup.sh' and 'make -C tests/fixtures'"
      exit 77
    fi
  done
}
===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T1: CSR insn-match switching mode (M1) + end-of-program (M8).
#
# Walks one round-trip with CSR triggers (csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}) on a
# state-rich Tier-2 fixture (rt_c_v_regs). Demonstrates the default
# switching path a user gets out of the box.
#
# Honors all the same env overrides as e2e.sh (VSIM_BIN,
# VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM, TIMEOUT).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

MODE_LABEL="CSR insn match (M1+M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
echo "Trigger: csrwi 0x7C0, 0 (enter) -> csrwi 0x7C0, 1 (exit)"
echo "=========================================================="

set +e
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/../e2e.sh"
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
  exit 77
fi

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
  exit "$RC"
fi

echo "[demo M1] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"

content.text: I have enough context. Let me ask you key design questions before finalizing the plan.

questions.question: What should the single end-user command be called and where should it live?

options.label: ./hsim at repo root (Recommended)
options.description: One shell command at the repo root: ./hsim build, ./hsim run app.elf, ./hsim test. Most discoverable - first thing user sees on git clone.

options.label: bin/hsim + PATH export
options.description: Lives in bin/hsim, exposed via source ./setup.sh so user can just type 'hsim ...' anywhere. Cleaner but hides the entry point.

options.label: ./sim at repo root
options.description: Shorter name. Same UX as ./hsim, just less specific (sim could mean anything).

questions.question: What language should the driver be written in?

options.label: Bash (Recommended)
options.description: Matches existing project style (setup.sh, e2e.sh, run_e2e.sh all bash). Zero extra deps. Easy to wrap existing scripts via env vars. Subcommand UX is uglier than Python.

options.label: Python 3
options.description: Better argparse-based --help per subcommand, JSON output, easier auto-detection logic (read ELF symbols via pyelftools). Adds a dep but Python is already required by the orchestrator.

questions.question: Should `hsim run` accept the user's own ELF, or only registered fixtures?

options.label: Both: fixtures by name + arbitrary ELF path (Recommended)
options.description: hsim run rt_c_hello (resolves to tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf) AND hsim run /path/to/my.elf. Auto-detects trigger mode by inspecting symbols/insns. This is the real 'I want to run my binary' UX.

options.label: Only registered fixtures (by name)
options.description: Simpler. hsim run rt_c_hello only. User must add a fixture entry to run their own code. Matches current workflow but less convenient.

options.label: Only arbitrary ELF paths
options.description: hsim run path/to/foo.elf only. Forces explicit path everywhere; no auto-resolve for fixtures.

questions.question: If artifacts are missing when the user runs `hsim run` or `hsim test`, what should happen?

options.label: Fail with one-line hint (Recommended)
options.description: Print 'vsim not built; run: ./hsim build' and exit non-zero. Predictable; user controls when long-running builds happen. Matches current paths.sh skip-77 behavior.

options.label: Auto-build silently then run
options.description: Implicitly run setup.sh + make -C tests/fixtures if anything is missing. Friendliest first-run UX but can surprise user with a 5-min build.

options.label: Auto-build with prompt
options.description: Detect missing artifacts, ask 'Build now? [y/N]'. Interactive; doesn't fit non-tty CI use.

aiTitle: Design main driver for end-user simplicity

questions.question: Which subcommands should the initial v1 of ./hsim ship with?

options.label: build [--what qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|fixtures|all]
options.description: Wraps setup.sh + 'make -C tests/fixtures'. Single entry point for all build needs.

options.label: run <elf|fixture> [--mode auto|csr|pc]
options.description: Wraps tests/e2e.sh. Auto-detects mode by scanning ELF for csrwi 0x7C0 or _hybrid_enter_pc symbol. Hides shared mmap, three-phase plumbing, log files.

options.label: test [--filter regex] [--mode csr|pc|both]
options.description: Wraps scripts/run_e2e.sh with JOBS=8 cap pre-set. Prints clean PASS/FAIL summary, points to log dir only on failure.

options.label: list [fixtures|demos|modes]
options.description: Lists what's available so user doesn't have to grep the Makefile. e.g. 'hsim list fixtures' -> table of all .elfs with one-line descriptions.

options.label: demo <T1..T7>
options.description: Wraps tests/demos/demo_*.sh so user can run individual demos by short name.

options.label: doctor
options.description: Diagnoses environment: checks HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN, all 4 binaries built, /dev/shm space, etc. Single command to see why something isn't working.

questions.question: How loud should `./hsim run` output be by default?

options.label: Quiet by default + --verbose flag (Recommended)
options.description: Print: phase-1/2/3 progress dots, final PASS/FAIL with exit code and elapsed time. On failure, point to log file. --verbose dumps the underlying QEMU/vsim stdout/stderr inline. Best signal-to-noise for end users.

options.label: Match current e2e.sh verbosity
options.description: Print every phase header like '[FIXTURE 1/3 mode=csr] QEMU 1: drain entry state'. Familiar to developers; noisier for newcomers.

options.label: Silent by default + --verbose flag
options.description: Print only the final PASS/FAIL line. --verbose for everything. Cleanest but unsettling during the 30-60s vsim phase.

questions.question: Should the auto-detection in `hsim run` cover both CSR insns AND PC symbols, and what if neither is found?

options.label: Try CSR insn match first, fall back to PC symbol, fail if neither (Recommended)
options.description: Disassemble .text for csrwi 0x7C0; if absent, look for _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc; if neither, fail with explicit message listing what to add. Mirrors current TRIGGER_MODE choice.

options.label: Require explicit --mode flag
options.description: No auto-detection; user must pass --mode csr|pc. Simpler driver code but pushes a developer-level choice onto users.

options.label: Default to CSR mode unless --mode pc given
options.description: Same as current e2e.sh default. Bug-prone if user has a PC-only binary.

file.content:
# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

Two submodules:

- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
- `verilator/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
for the full list.

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `QEMU/README.rst`)

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `QEMU/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `verilator/build/vsim`

input.content:
# Plan: `./hsim` end-user driver

## Context

The hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim) today exposes a developer-grade
surface: `source ./setup.sh`, then `make -C tests/fixtures`, then
`JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`, or per-test
`FIXTURE=... TRIGGER_MODE=... bash tests/e2e.sh`. End users have to
know seven env vars (`JOBS`, `MODE`, `FILTER`, `TRIGGER_MODE`,
`TIMEOUT`, `EXPECT_RC`, `LOG_DIR`), the right `JOBS=8` cap on a 128-core
host, how to source `setup.sh`, the two-step build, and the fact that
the README's `./build.sh` reference is stale.

The goal: ship one entry point, `./hsim`, that any new user can run
without reading docs first. It must hide build/path/mmap/log-file
plumbing and call into the existing scripts -- no reimplementation,
no logic duplication.

User decisions captured (from clarifying questions):

- Name + location: **`./hsim` at repo root** (most discoverable).
- Language: **Python 3** (already a host dep; better argparse UX).
- Run scope: **fixtures by name AND arbitrary ELF paths**.
- Missing artifacts: **fail with one-line hint** -- no auto-build.
- v1 subcommands: **build, run, test, list** (drop demo/doctor for v1).
- Output: **quiet + `--verbose`** flag.
- Trigger mode: **require explicit `--mode csr|pc`** (no auto-detect in v1).

## Architecture

```
./hsim  (Python 3 stdlib only, no pip deps)
  |
  +-- build [--what qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|fixtures|all]
  |     -> subprocess: bash setup.sh        (artifacts 1-4)
  |     -> subprocess: make -C tests/fixtures   (fixtures)
  |
  +-- run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc [--vlen 512] [--timeout 60] [-v]
  |     -> subprocess: FIXTURE=... TRIGGER_MODE=... bash tests/e2e.sh
  |        (with VSIM_ELF override when arg is an arbitrary path)
  |
  +-- test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]
  |     -> subprocess: JOBS=<N or 8> MODE=... FILTER=... bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
  |
  +-- list <fixtures|demos|modes>
        -> parse tests/fixtures/Makefile, ls tests/demos/, hardcoded M1..M8 table
```

Path resolution (mirrors `tests/lib/paths.sh::hybrid_resolve_paths`):

- `VSIM_QEMU = build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `VSIM_BIN  = verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium`
- `VSIM_PLUGIN = qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `SIMPOINT_BIN = tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
- Read `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` from `config.env` if not in env.

For each subcommand that needs artifacts, check presence up front and
exit 2 with one-line hint: `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.

## Interface (input / output contract)

```
./hsim build                          # build everything
./hsim build --what vsim              # rebuild only vsim
./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr      # fixture by name
./hsim run my/app.elf --mode pc       # arbitrary ELF path
./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode csr -v  # verbose: dump QEMU/vsim stdout
./hsim test                           # JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
./hsim test --filter rt_c_v --mode csr
./hsim list fixtures                  # table of fixture names + family
./hsim list modes                     # M1..M8 short table
./hsim --help                         # global help; per-subcommand --help also
```

Output style:

- `run` (default quiet):
    ```
    [hsim] rt_c_hello mode=csr vlen=512
    [1/3] QEMU1 drain ......... ok (1.2s)
    [2/3] vsim ROI execute .... ok (4.7s)
    [3/3] QEMU2 resume ........ ok (0.4s)
    PASS rt_c_hello in 6.3s
    ```
- On failure: print the underlying log path at the end so user can `cat`
  it; do not dump 100+ lines of SystemC noise inline (unless `-v`).
- `test` (default quiet): suppress per-test phase output; print a
  final summary `PASS 32, FAIL 0, SKIP 0  (total 51.2s)`; on failure
  also print the per-test log paths.

Exit codes:

- 0: success
- 1: test/run failure (functional)
- 2: missing artifact, bad arg, env not set (configuration)
- 77: skip (artifact missing in CTest-style contexts; preserved for
  compatibility with `tests/lib/paths.sh`)

## Files

**Create:**
- `hsim` (executable Python 3 script, ~250-350 lines, stdlib only).
  Lives at `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hsim`. Single file -- no
  package directory until v2. argparse with subparsers.
- `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` -- RED test for `list fixtures` (TDD).
- `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` -- RED test for `build --dry-run`
  prints the underlying commands without executing.
- `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` -- RED test that
  `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 (requires built artifacts;
  matches existing skip-77 convention).

**Modify:**
- `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh` block with `./hsim`
  quickstart (3 lines: build, run, test). The Host deps + Outputs
  sections stay.
- `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new "Section 0.5: Quick start with
  `./hsim`" that shows the four subcommands only. The existing
  Sections 1-7 stay for developers who want the raw env-var surface.
- `CLAUDE.md` (repo) -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build` and
  `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark the raw scripts as
  "developer escape hatch".

**Leverage unchanged (per "don't duplicate code"):**
- `setup.sh` -- still does all artifact builds; `hsim build` just calls it.
- `tests/fixtures/Makefile` -- `hsim build --what fixtures` just runs
  `make -C tests/fixtures` with `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` from `config.env`.
- `tests/e2e.sh` -- `hsim run` passes `FIXTURE`, `TRIGGER_MODE`,
  `EXPECT_RC`, `TIMEOUT`, and (for arbitrary ELF) `VSIM_ELF`.
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh` -- `hsim test` passes `JOBS`, `MODE`, `FILTER`.
- `tests/lib/paths.sh` -- logic mirrored in Python (`Paths` dataclass)
  but the bash version is still source-of-truth for the harness.

## Key functions to reuse from the codebase

- `tests/lib/paths.sh:hybrid_resolve_paths` (logic, not file) -- exit
  code 77 for skip, the four env var defaults.
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh:default_jobs()` -- already caps JOBS sensibly;
  `hsim test` just passes through `JOBS=8` unless user overrides.
- `setup.sh::_hybrid_setup_main` -- supports `clean [target]` already;
  `hsim build --clean [target]` maps directly to this.

## Implementation order (TDD: RED -> GREEN per subcommand)

Per the TDD gate in user CLAUDE.md, each subcommand follows
RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR with structural and behavioral commits
separated. Build one subcommand at a time:

1. **`list`** (lowest risk -- pure parsing, no subprocess):
   - RED: `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` asserts that
     `./hsim list fixtures` outputs lines containing `rt_c_hello` and
     `rt_all_gprs`; assert `./hsim list modes` outputs `M1` and `M8`.
   - GREEN: parse `tests/fixtures/Makefile` for `.elf` targets;
     hardcode M1..M8 table from USER_GUIDE.md.
2. **`build`**:
   - RED: `--dry-run` flag prints `bash setup.sh` and
     `make -C tests/fixtures` without running.
   - GREEN: argparse subparser; subprocess.run with check=True.
   - Behavioral commit: actual exec, no --dry-run.
3. **`run`**:
   - RED: argparse rejects missing `--mode`; rejects unknown fixture;
     accepts arbitrary `.elf` path; exits 2 with hint when vsim binary
     missing.
   - GREEN: wire into `tests/e2e.sh` with the right env vars.
   - Quiet output formatting is a separate structural change.
4. **`test`**:
   - RED: `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` runs
     and reports `PASS 1, FAIL 0`.
   - GREEN: wrap `scripts/run_e2e.sh`, parse its results.tsv, print
     summary.

After all four pass: structural-only commit to refactor shared
`run_subprocess` / `Paths` helpers if duplication appears. Never
mix structural with behavioral in one commit.

## Verification (end-to-end)

Smoke sequence the maintainer can run after the driver lands:

```sh
# From a clean checkout, no env sourced:
./hsim                                 # prints help + 4 subcommands
./hsim list fixtures                   # exits 0, lists ~17 fixtures
./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr       # exits 2 with "run ./hsim build" hint
./hsim build                           # ~5 min on cold cache; exits 0
./hsim list fixtures                   # unchanged
./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr       # exits 0, PASS in ~6s
./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr -v    # same but with QEMU/vsim stdout
./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr   # arbitrary path
./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode pc       # uses _hybrid_enter_pc symbol
./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr   # runs ~7 tests with JOBS=8 cap
./hsim test                            # full suite, ~5 min, summary
```

Each line above is a checkbox the implementor must tick before
calling v1 done. The three `tests/hsim/test_hsim_*.sh` files give a
CI-grade automated version of the first 4-5 steps.

## Out of scope for v1 (deferred)

- `demo <T1..T7>` subcommand (wrap `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`).
- `doctor` env-health subcommand.
- Auto-detect trigger mode by ELF inspection (`--mode auto`).
- Building user's own ELF -- v1 only RUNS user binaries that are
  already hybrid-compatible (have CSR triggers or `_hybrid_enter_pc`).
- Bash-completion file for the subcommand names.
- M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) -- v1 covers M1 (csr)
  and M2 (pc) only, matching the only modes `tests/e2e.sh` supports
  today. Other modes stay reachable via the existing demo scripts.

## Approved Plan:
# Plan: `./hsim` end-user driver

## Context

The hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim) today exposes a developer-grade
surface: `source ./setup.sh`, then `make -C tests/fixtures`, then
`JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`, or per-test
`FIXTURE=... TRIGGER_MODE=... bash tests/e2e.sh`. End users have to
know seven env vars (`JOBS`, `MODE`, `FILTER`, `TRIGGER_MODE`,
`TIMEOUT`, `EXPECT_RC`, `LOG_DIR`), the right `JOBS=8` cap on a 128-core
host, how to source `setup.sh`, the two-step build, and the fact that
the README's `./build.sh` reference is stale.

The goal: ship one entry point, `./hsim`, that any new user can run
without reading docs first. It must hide build/path/mmap/log-file
plumbing and call into the existing scripts -- no reimplementation,
no logic duplication.

User decisions captured (from clarifying questions):

- Name + location: **`./hsim` at repo root** (most discoverable).
- Language: **Python 3** (already a host dep; better argparse UX).
- Run scope: **fixtures by name AND arbitrary ELF paths**.
- Missing artifacts: **fail with one-line hint** -- no auto-build.
- v1 subcommands: **build, run, test, list** (drop demo/doctor for v1).
- Output: **quiet + `--verbose`** flag.
- Trigger mode: **require explicit `--mode csr|pc`** (no auto-detect in v1).

## Architecture

```
./hsim  (Python 3 stdlib only, no pip deps)
  |
  +-- build [--what qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|fixtures|all]
  |     -> subprocess: bash setup.sh        (artifacts 1-4)
  |     -> subprocess: make -C tests/fixtures   (fixtures)
  |
  +-- run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc [--vlen 512] [--timeout 60] [-v]
  |     -> subprocess: FIXTURE=... TRIGGER_MODE=... bash tests/e2e.sh
  |        (with VSIM_ELF override when arg is an arbitrary path)
  |
  +-- test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]
  |     -> subprocess: JOBS=<N or 8> MODE=... FILTER=... bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
  |
  +-- list <fixtures|demos|modes>
        -> parse tests/fixtures/Makefile, ls tests/demos/, hardcoded M1..M8 table
```

Path resolution (mirrors `tests/lib/paths.sh::hybrid_resolve_paths`):

- `VSIM_QEMU = build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `VSIM_BIN  = verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium`
- `VSIM_PLUGIN = qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `SIMPOINT_BIN = tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
- Read `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` from `config.env` if not in env.

For each subcommand that needs artifacts, check presence up front and
exit 2 with one-line hint: `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.

## Interface (input / output contract)

```
./hsim build                          # build everything
./hsim build --what vsim              # rebuild only vsim
./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr      # fixture by name
./hsim run my/app.elf --mode pc       # arbitrary ELF path
./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode csr -v  # verbose: dump QEMU/vsim stdout
./hsim test                           # JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
./hsim test --filter rt_c_v --mode csr
./hsim list fixtures                  # table of fixture names + family
./hsim list modes                     # M1..M8 short table
./hsim --help                         # global help; per-subcommand --help also
```

Output style:

- `run` (default quiet):
    ```
    [hsim] rt_c_hello mode=csr vlen=512
    [1/3] QEMU1 drain ......... ok (1.2s)
    [2/3] vsim ROI execute .... ok (4.7s)
    [3/3] QEMU2 resume ........ ok (0.4s)
    PASS rt_c_hello in 6.3s
    ```
- On failure: print the underlying log path at the end so user can `cat`
  it; do not dump 100+ lines of SystemC noise inline (unless `-v`).
- `test` (default quiet): suppress per-test phase output; print a
  final summary `PASS 32, FAIL 0, SKIP 0  (total 51.2s)`; on failure
  also print the per-test log paths.

Exit codes:

- 0: success
- 1: test/run failure (functional)
- 2: missing artifact, bad arg, env not set (configuration)
- 77: skip (artifact missing in CTest-style contexts; preserved for
  compatibility with `tests/lib/paths.sh`)

## Files

**Create:**
- `hsim` (executable Python 3 script, ~250-350 lines, stdlib only).
  Lives at `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hsim`. Single file -- no
  package directory until v2. argparse with subparsers.
- `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` -- RED test for `list fixtures` (TDD).
- `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` -- RED test for `build --dry-run`
  prints the underlying commands without executing.
- `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` -- RED test that
  `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 (requires built artifacts;
  matches existing skip-77 convention).

**Modify:**
- `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh` block with `./hsim`
  quickstart (3 lines: build, run, test). The Host deps + Outputs
  sections stay.
- `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new "Section 0.5: Quick start with
  `./hsim`" that shows the four subcommands only. The existing
  Sections 1-7 stay for developers who want the raw env-var surface.
- `CLAUDE.md` (repo) -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build` and
  `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark the raw scripts as
  "developer escape hatch".

**Leverage unchanged (per "don't duplicate code"):**
- `setup.sh` -- still does all artifact builds; `hsim build` just calls it.
- `tests/fixtures/Makefile` -- `hsim build --what fixtures` just runs
  `make -C tests/fixtures` with `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` from `config.env`.
- `tests/e2e.sh` -- `hsim run` passes `FIXTURE`, `TRIGGER_MODE`,
  `EXPECT_RC`, `TIMEOUT`, and (for arbitrary ELF) `VSIM_ELF`.
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh` -- `hsim test` passes `JOBS`, `MODE`, `FILTER`.
- `tests/lib/paths.sh` -- logic mirrored in Python (`Paths` dataclass)

## Key functions to reuse from the codebase

content.content:
# ROADMAP: Relocate cross-cutting e2e tests to top-level `hybrid_sim/`

Tracks the migration of the e2e test tree (fixtures, harness, drivers, plugin
shell tests, demos) out of the `verilator/` submodule and into the top-level
`hybrid_sim/` repo, so the wire format (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) and the
tests that exercise it live in the same repo.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: structural+behavioral commits landed and Phase-0 baseline pass list matches

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == /tmp/baseline-pass.txt from F0
```

Any divergence blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F0 ----> F1 ----> F2 ----> F3 ----> F4 ----> F5 ----> F6 ----> F7
                  (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR
                   sub-loop inside F2)
```

Strict serial order from F1..F6. F7 (docs) can land any time after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (answer before F1)

1. **History preservation** - cross-repo `cp` loses per-file `git blame` in the
   new home. If blame matters, do F1 via `git filter-repo --path hybrid/test
   --path tests/hybrid` on a clone of `verilator` and merge with
   `--allow-unrelated-histories`. Adds ~half a day. Default: plain copy,
   accept that blame for moved files starts fresh in `hybrid_sim/`.
2. **Submodule CI** - any pipeline in `verilator` that runs
   `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` silently passes with 0 tests after F4. Decide:
   delete the CI job, or have it clone the parent repo.
3. **`runtime/` headers** - confirm `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h`
   under `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/` are standalone fixture files, not
   sync-mirrors of vsim CPU description headers. If they mirror, add a
   regenerate step or keep them in the submodule.

## Features

### F0 - Capture baseline pass list

- Status: done
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Snapshot the current green test list so every later phase can
  diff against it. Runs both CSR and PC trigger modes.
- Steps:
  ```
  JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-baseline.log
  sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | grep '^\[ok\]'   | sort > /tmp/baseline-pass.txt
  sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | grep '^\[FAIL\]' | sort > /tmp/baseline-fail.txt
  ```
  (sed strips ANSI color codes the runner emits.)
- Output: `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt` is the diff target for F1..F6.
- Result (captured 2026-05-18): 42 PASS / 3 FAIL.
  - The 3 failing tests were pre-existing breakage orthogonal to this
    migration: `rt_no_qemu_binary`, `rt_gdbstub_timeout`, `rt_pty_handback`
    used `-M virt -kernel` against fixtures linked for `andes_ae350`,
    so QEMU1 never reached the `csrwi` trigger and the drivers hung
    until killed. F1..F6 only needed to preserve the 42 PASS; the 3 FAIL
    stayed FAIL through the migration.
  - Post-roadmap follow-up [a40bd3f]: switched the 3 drivers (plus
    `tests/plugin/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh` and
    `tests/plugin/probe_vcsr_drain.sh`) to
    `-machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem -bios`. New baseline:
    **45 PASS / 0 FAIL**.

input.content:
# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver

Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
```

`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
                                                            |
                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
```

Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)

1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.

## Features

### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`

- Status: in-progress
- Type: behavioral (new code)
- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
- Key files:
  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: none.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.

### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
    fixtures and exits 0.
  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
  the right env vars. Resolves fixture-by-name (`rt_c_hello` ->
  `tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf`) AND arbitrary ELF paths
  (`my/app.elf`). Quiet 3-line progress output by default;
  `--verbose` dumps QEMU/vsim stdout inline.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
    quiet/verbose output formatter).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello` (no `--mode`) exits 2 with usage hint.
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` on a clean build exits 0 in <30s.
  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` when vsim binary is absent
    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
  - `--verbose` prints the underlying e2e.sh stderr+stdout.

### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented
  but is demoted to "escape hatch" sections.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`
    quickstart block (lines 12-22) with `./hsim` quickstart.
  - **Modify:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new
    "Section 0.5: Quick start with `./hsim`" before the existing
    Section 1 build instructions. Keep Sections 1-7 unchanged.
  - **Modify:** `CLAUDE.md` -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build`
    and `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark raw scripts as
    developer escape hatch.
- Dependencies: F1..F4 (so the examples in docs work).
- Done criteria:
  - README quickstart is 3 lines and uses only `./hsim` commands.
  - USER_GUIDE Section 0.5 has a copy-pastable 4-command worked
    example.
  - CLAUDE.md still lists the underlying scripts for developers but
    points to `./hsim` first.

### F6 - End-to-end verification

- Status: not-started
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
  clean checkout. Tick each step.
- Steps:
  ```
  ./hsim                                                  # help
  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # ~17 fixtures
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # exit 2, hint
  ./hsim build                                            # ~5 min cold
  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # unchanged
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # PASS ~6s
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr -v                     # verbose
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr    # arbitrary path
  ./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode pc                        # PC trigger
  ./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr                    # ~7 tests
  ./hsim test                                             # full suite
  ```
- Dependencies: F1..F5.
- Done criteria: all 11 steps exit as expected; the full-suite line
  reports the same 45 PASS / 0 FAIL as the e2e baseline.

## Out of scope (deferred to a future driver_ROADMAP_v2)

- `./hsim demo <T1..T7>` -- wrap `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`.
- `./hsim doctor` -- env-health diagnoser.
- `./hsim run --mode auto` -- auto-detect via ELF inspection.
- Building user's own non-hybrid-compatible ELF (instrument it for them).
- M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) -- currently only
  reachable via the demo scripts.
- Bash-completion file.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F1: `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`.
#
# Asserts the discoverability surface of the new driver. Does not need
# any artifacts built; pure parsing.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"

# fixtures: must include at least one asm fixture and one C fixture.
out=$("$HSIM" list fixtures 2>&1) || fail "list fixtures exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw rt_all_gprs || fail "list fixtures missing rt_all_gprs:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw rt_c_hello  || fail "list fixtures missing rt_c_hello:\n$out"
ok "list fixtures includes rt_all_gprs and rt_c_hello"

# demos: must include the M1 (csr) and M2 (pc) demos.
out=$("$HSIM" list demos 2>&1) || fail "list demos exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw demo_csr_oneshot || fail "list demos missing demo_csr_oneshot:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw demo_pc_oneshot  || fail "list demos missing demo_pc_oneshot:\n$out"
ok "list demos includes demo_csr_oneshot and demo_pc_oneshot"

# modes: must include M1 and M8 from USER_GUIDE.
out=$("$HSIM" list modes 2>&1) || fail "list modes exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw M1 || fail "list modes missing M1:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw M8 || fail "list modes missing M8:\n$out"
ok "list modes includes M1 and M8"

# bogus arg: must exit non-zero with a usage hint.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" list bogus 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -ne 0 ] || fail "list bogus should exit non-zero, got 0; output: $out"
ok "list bogus exits non-zero (rc=$rc)"

echo "PASS test_hsim_list"

2	#
3	# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
4	# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
5	# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want

11	endif
12	CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
13	LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
14	OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump
15	
16	CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
17	LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static
18	
19	# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
20	RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
21	               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
22	               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival
23	
24	# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
25	# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
26	# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
27	# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
28	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
29	# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
30	# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
31	# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
32	RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
33	RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
34	# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
35	# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
36	PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
37	
38	RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
39	RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
40	                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
41	# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
42	# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
43	# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
44	# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
45	RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
46	# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
47	# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
48	# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
49	# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
50	# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
51	RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
52	RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
53	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
54	# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
55	# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
56	RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
57	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
#
# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
# to bypass the env var.

TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
endif
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump demo

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver

Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
```

`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
                                                            |
                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
```

Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)

1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.

## Features

### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`

- Status: in-progress
- Type: behavioral (new code)
- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
- Key files:
  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: none.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.

### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
    fixtures and exits 0.
  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
  the right env vars. Resolves fixture-by-name (`rt_c_hello` ->
  `tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf`) AND arbitrary ELF paths
  (`my/app.elf`). Quiet 3-line progress output by default;
  `--verbose` dumps QEMU/vsim stdout inline.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
    quiet/verbose output formatter).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello` (no `--mode`) exits 2 with usage hint.
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` on a clean build exits 0 in <30s.
  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` when vsim binary is absent
    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
  - `--verbose` prints the underlying e2e.sh stderr+stdout.

### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented
  but is demoted to "escape hatch" sections.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`
    quickstart block (lines 12-22) with `./hsim` quickstart.
  - **Modify:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new
    "Section 0.5: Quick start with `./hsim`" before the existing
    Section 1 build instructions. Keep Sections 1-7 unchanged.
  - **Modify:** `CLAUDE.md` -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build`
    and `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark raw scripts as
    developer escape hatch.
- Dependencies: F1..F4 (so the examples in docs work).
- Done criteria:
  - README quickstart is 3 lines and uses only `./hsim` commands.
  - USER_GUIDE Section 0.5 has a copy-pastable 4-command worked
    example.
  - CLAUDE.md still lists the underlying scripts for developers but
    points to `./hsim` first.

### F6 - End-to-end verification

- Status: not-started
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
  clean checkout. Tick each step.
- Steps:
  ```
  ./hsim                                                  # help
  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # ~17 fixtures
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # exit 2, hint
  ./hsim build                                            # ~5 min cold
  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # unchanged
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # PASS ~6s
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr -v                     # verbose
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr    # arbitrary path
  ./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode pc                        # PC trigger
  ./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr                    # ~7 tests
  ./hsim test                                             # full suite
  ```
- Dependencies: F1..F5.
- Done criteria: all 11 steps exit as expected; the full-suite line
  reports the same 45 PASS / 0 FAIL as the e2e baseline.

## Out of scope (deferred to a future driver_ROADMAP_v2)

- `./hsim demo <T1..T7>` -- wrap `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`.
- `./hsim doctor` -- env-health diagnoser.
- `./hsim run --mode auto` -- auto-detect via ELF inspection.
- Building user's own non-hybrid-compatible ELF (instrument it for them).
- M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) -- currently only
  reachable via the demo scripts.
- Bash-completion file.
